goal: Fix: mainline start uses merge-base with main when HEAD is ahead, so commit-before-start no longer empties the seal diff

summary.title: mainline start uses merge-base with main as BaseCommit when HEAD is ahead
summary.what: When `mainline start` runs and HEAD is strictly ahead of the synced main ref, set the new draft's BaseCommit to the merge-base instead of HEAD. Pre-start commits on the same branch are now visible to `seal --prepare`'s diff, so the starter's fingerprint.files_touched populates correctly. Falls back to HEAD on the existing happy path (no commits yet, no remote, merge-base lookup error). Backfill flow's explicit base override still wins.
summary.why: Trial-user footgun: a literal reading of the README quickstart can run `git commit` before `mainline start`. With base=HEAD, the diff seal --prepare reports is empty, the starter's fingerprint.files_touched is empty, and the validator rejects on `fingerprint.files_touched must not be empty`. The fix lets the documented order work AND the alternate order work. Both are now valid trial paths.
summary.user_goal: A trial user who runs `git commit` before `mainline start` can still complete the seal flow without `--allow-dirty` and without manually editing fingerprint.files_touched.

decisions.point: fix at start time, not at prepare time
decisions.chose: Set BaseCommit correctly when the draft is first written.
decisions.rationale: Prepare reads the draft's BaseCommit and computes the diff. Fixing it at start means a single source of truth — every subsequent prepare/append/seal sees the same base. Fixing it at prepare time would mean re-deriving the base on every prepare call and risking inconsistency with what start recorded.

decisions.point: only adopt merge-base when HEAD > main
decisions.chose: Check that mainHead != currentHead before doing the merge-base lookup; only adopt the result when it's strictly behind HEAD.
decisions.rationale: The no-commits-yet path (HEAD == main HEAD) must keep base = HEAD so subsequent commits ARE in the diff. Adopting the merge-base unconditionally would break that case.
Backfill is a different contract — the user is naming specific commits to claim. The merge-base heuristic shouldn't override an explicit base.

rejected.alternative: Detect the empty-diff case at seal --submit time and return a friendlier error.
rejected.reason: Better error messages help, but the actual fix is to make the documented loop work in both orders. Friendly errors are downstream of the logic fix.

rejected.alternative: Always use main HEAD as BaseCommit.
rejected.reason: Branches with multiple intents would silently re-claim each other's commits — every new intent would diff from main, not from the prior intent's seal. The merge-base approach preserves intent-per-branch boundaries on the standard PR flow while fixing the commit-before-start case.

rejected.alternative: Add a CLI hint when start is called on a branch ahead of main.
rejected.reason: Hints don't change behaviour; the broken-flow case still fails. Fixing the logic is strictly better.

risks: If a user has a long-lived feature branch with multiple sealed intents, calling start on a fresh empty-diff state will set base = merge-base with main, NOT base = head of the previous intent's seal. Subsequent prepare's diff would include all commits since branch divergence, not just the new intent's work. Mitigation: this is the same behaviour the existing seal flow has when seal --prepare is called against a long branch — the user is expected to either (a) seal frequently or (b) accept that intent-per-branch is the supported pattern. Documented in spec §3.

anti_patterns.what: Computing BaseCommit at prepare time instead of start time
anti_patterns.why: Prepare must be deterministic given the draft state. Re-deriving the base on every prepare call risks the base shifting under the user's feet between prepare and submit.
On a branch with no commits yet, merge-base equals HEAD; setting base = merge-base while keeping HEAD-comparison logic produces an empty diff for the legitimate no-work-yet case.

followups: Document the chosen order in the README (currently 'start → edit → commit → seal'). Either order works now, but the recommended order remains unchanged for predictability.

Consider warning at seal --submit time when fingerprint.files_touched is empty AND the user did NOT pass `--allow-dirty` — point at this fix as the typical cause if it's a long branch with multiple intents.

Investigate whether long-lived branches with multiple intents need a more nuanced base (parent intent's seal commit?) — but only if user reports the friction; intent-per-branch is the supported pattern today.

architectural_claims: BaseCommit is decided at start time and recorded on the draft; subsequent prepare/append/seal calls read it as a fixed input.
merge-base with synced main is the right base for a branch with pre-start commits; HEAD is the right base when HEAD == main HEAD.
behavioral_changes: `mainline start` on a branch whose HEAD is ahead of main now sets BaseCommit to the merge-base instead of HEAD.
Seal flow now succeeds for the commit-before-start order on a fresh feature branch.
migration_notes: Existing drafts on disk are unchanged; only newly-started drafts pick up the new base logic.
